description: Accounts of a grain merchant, probably. In Judaeo-Arabic. Customers include ʿAbdallāh, al-Mujāhid, Muhannā, and the Muʿallaqa (presumably the church). The grain is sold by the wayba, irdabb, tillīs, and baṭṭa. It is referred to once as ground (maṭḥūn) and once as flour (daqīq). There seems to be a variety called כשכאר.
description_he: רשימה או טבלה ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בMTA.
